slides.title: Overview
slides.content: Six contacts. Multiple vectors. One Shahed that laughs at your jammer. Module 4 is where operators learn that C-UAS is a resource management problem as much as a targeting problem. Waste JACKAL rounds on jammable targets and you will run dry.

slides.title: Learning Objectives
content: Manage effector economy — conserve JACKAL rounds, sequence jammer recharges
Recognize and correctly respond to a Shahed-style autonomous threat (jam-immune)
Coordinate simultaneous engagements across multiple effector types
Maintain awareness of all tracks while actively engaging 2+ contacts

slides.title: Key Concepts
content: Autonomous/INS-navigated threats: jam-immune, kinetic defeat only
Layered defense: EW as first resort, kinetic as last
Jammer mutual exclusion — active jamming can affect friendly GPS
Swarm tactics: saturation and prioritization under cognitive load

slides.title: Standards
content: Shahed correctly identified as jam-immune; JACKAL or kinetic used
≤2 JACKAL rounds wasted on jammable targets
No hostile track reaches the protected area unengaged
